mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals rising aggregate labor income and consumption momentum, which drives upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% signals a zero lower bound monetary regime that suppresses short-term discount rates and risk-free alternatives, channeling capital into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal robust consumer demand financed by accommodative credit conditions, amplifying cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A two-factor model including the interaction of RSAFS momentum and FEDFUNDS level fails to achieve a delta R-squared above 0.01 relative to individual main effects over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is between 4.5% and 6.5% and rising from its trailing 12-month low, increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal late-cycle labor market deterioration that precedes demand destruction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases while between 4.5% and 6.5% and above its 12-month low
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger condition, rising UNRATE coincides with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which translates into higher operating leverage for cyclical firms and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 2.5% signals inflation overshoot that raises expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, increasing the equity discount rate and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns in triggered months are positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of month-over-month growth in housing starts (HOUST) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates broad-based strength in construction and labor markets that drives cyclical earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and falling UNRATE underperforms the unconditional S&P 500 60-day baseline by more than 200 basis points annualized over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in real GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter growth during the first eight quarters of an economic expansion signals escaping velocity from recession, triggering equity multiple expansion and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q growth accelerates versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Following GDPC1 accelerations within the first 8 quarters of expansion, S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.
